steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Sentence 2 says Loske analyzes 50 palettes. Sentence 3 says she reveals clues about specific artists. The transition must show that the revealing is the result of the analyzing — a cause-and-effect link, not a contrast or a sequence.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — However,: signals contrast or contradiction. Sentence 3 does not contradict sentence 2; it follows from it. ✗
  • Choice B — In comparison,: signals that two things are being compared side by side. No comparison is being made here. ✗
  • Choice C — Lastly,: signals the final item in a series. The passage is not listing steps; it is showing what the analysis accomplishes. ✗
  • Choice D — In doing so,: refers back to the action just described (analyzing 50 palettes) and signals that the next sentence states what that action achieves — a clean cause-and-effect bridge. ✓
That matches Choice D — In doing so,.
